mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor income and consumer spending, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This relationship holds consistently across all regimes without any threshold condition.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% in Sep 2007, low by historical standards but rising from trough
failure_condition: If the 12-month correlation between monthly UNRATE changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is positive (rank IC > 0.05) or if the hit rate for negative prediction when UNRATE increases exceeds 50% over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.94% in Sep 2007, just below threshold
failure_condition: If over any two-year period when FEDFUNDS > 5%, the average 20-day forward return is not negative or is not significantly different from periods with FEDFUNDS <= 5% at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices coupled with increasing unemployment create a stagflationary environment that erodes both corporate margins and demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The joint condition of both rising is required; each alone is less reliable.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 2% and UNRATE month-over-month change > 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 207.7 in Aug 2007, moderate inflation ~2.2% YoY
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return in months meeting the trigger condition is not significantly negative (t-test p < 0.10) compared to non-trigger months, or if the interaction term in a linear regression of CPIAUCSL and UNRATE on returns has a coefficient of zero.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing market contraction, declining housing starts signal a severe reduction in residential investment and associated consumer spending, which depresses overall economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. This mechanism is only active when housing starts are below a threshold indicating recessionary conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1331 in Aug 2007, down from peak of 2273 in Jan 2006
failure_condition: If at any point when HOUST is below 1500, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of instances, or if the predictive rank IC is below 0.05 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index indicates expanding manufacturing output, which reflects broad economic strength and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The relationship is monotonic across all regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 114.4 in Aug 2007, modest growth from year ago
failure_condition: If the 3-year rolling correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is negative, or if the average forward return in months with INDPRO above its 12-month moving average is not significantly higher than months below.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in total nonfarm payrolls is a powerful recession indicator, immediately signaling falling labor demand and rising economic distress, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138,265 in Sep 2007, still growing but potential turn
failure_condition: If in months following a negative PAYEMS change, the 5-day forward SPX return is not negative on average, or if the strategy of shorting when triggered does not achieve a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer spending (retail sales) is high and unemployment is very low, it suggests an overheating economy where the Fed may tighten aggressively, leading to a future correction. The combination of high consumption and tight labor market is a leading indicator of negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 380 in Sep 2007 (below 400k, condition not met)
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return following trigger is not significantly negative (p<0.10), or if the interaction term has no predictive power in a logit model for direction.

mechanism_narrative: In the late-cycle expansion regime, a quarter-over-quarter slowdown in real GDP growth warns of approaching recession. As GDP growth decelerates, forward-looking equities price in weaker earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. This signal is only reliable when the economy has been expanding for several years.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11520.1 in Q1 2007, growth rate ~1.1% annualized, down from previous quarters
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (economy in expansion for at least 5 years)
failure_condition: If the signal of GDP growth below 1% annualized is not followed by negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of instances, or if the predictive accuracy does not exceed 50% in out-of-sample tests.
